title: Invoica × x402 Facilitator Integration — Arb Bot Repurposing
audit_note: Arb bot code audited 2026-03-10 from Arb-bot-main/ in Invoica folder. Bug descriptions updated to reflect actual code state.
architecture_note: Facilitator-agnostic. verifyPayAI() replaced by verifyFacilitator() abstraction backed by on-chain registry. PayAI is first in registry with 1.2x reputation weight boost for 18 months. Coinbase and any future x402 facilitator joins registry — zero code change. Routes renamed /payai/ → /x402/.

human_gate.action: PRE-001 — Register 3 Invoica services at payai.network for marketplace distribution. Not a technical blocker — services are facilitator-agnostic.
human_gate._restructured: 2026-03-10: PRE-001 demoted from technical blocker to distribution-only. Services are facilitator-agnostic — can build/deploy/test with mock or self-hosted facilitator. PayAI registration only needed for marketplace listing (MKT-001).

tasks.title: x402 Partner Registration — Human Action
tasks._agent_note: Rejected by agent 2026-03-10 — awaiting human action. Tarek must manually contact PayAI and complete registration before this can proceed.
tasks.escalation: If PRE-001 is not completed by 2026-03-17, CEO to escalate via alternative channel (direct DM to PayAI founder on X/Telegram) and evaluate whether to proceed with Coinbase as launch facilitator instead. All downstream tasks (PAY-001, INFRA-002, TREAS-001 via human_gate) are blocked on this.
tasks.description: Two parts: (1) Register 3 Invoica services on PayAI partner portal. Use investor relationship for direct access. Get X402_SERVICE_ID_ROUTER, X402_SERVICE_ID_TREASURY, X402_SERVICE_ID_ORACLE. Also get PAYAI_VERIFY_URL (PayAI's x402 /verify endpoint — needed by INFRA-002 to seed the registry). Negotiate 50% fee reduction for PayAI users first 3 months. (2) Register PayAI as the first facilitator in the on-chain facilitator registry once INFRA-002 is deployed — provide PAYAI_VERIFY_URL and service IDs to CTO. Add all values to .env.example.
tasks._restructured: 2026-03-10: PRE-001 no longer gates PAY-001 or INFRA-002. Services are facilitator-agnostic — deploy with test facilitator, add PayAI to registry later.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-01 CRITICAL: Rotate exposed private key + .gitignore
tasks._agent_note: COMPLETED 2026-03-10. New keypair generated (pubkey: 8Tcako8K8cuzm9CbJJ9VRjRpWCRwk8kZYAa6b7aHHA73). Old key scrubbed from git history via filter-repo + force push. Arb-bot-main/wallet/ added to .gitignore. Old wallet (7813VFF...) verified empty — no funds to drain.
tasks.description: AUDITED: wallet/keypair.json contains a REAL 64-byte Solana private key committed to the repo (confirmed: [245,255,200,179,...]). This is a live key, not a placeholder. Three steps: 1) Add wallet/keypair.json to .gitignore immediately. 2) ROTATE THE KEY — generate new keypair, transfer any funds, never use the exposed key again. 3) Document key rotation in README. This must be the very first task — do not commit any other changes to this repo before the key is rotated.
tasks.verification_deliverable: After rotation, write the NEW wallet public key (base58) to docs/key-rotation-log.md with date. Supervisor must confirm: (1) old pubkey no longer appears in any committed file, (2) wallet/keypair.json is in .gitignore, (3) new pubkey matches the key used in README. This is the only way to prove rotation actually happened.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-02: Replace CPMM quotes with DLMM swapQuote() for SOL legs
tasks.description: AUDITED: quotes.ts uses getAmountOut() (CPMM x*y=k formula) for SOL↔A and SOL↔B legs (lines 85-99). execute.ts correctly uses pool.swapQuote() for actual execution. This inconsistency means the quote engine shows phantom profit margins that don't match real execution prices — root cause of chasing false arbitrage. Fix: replace getAmountOut() calls in calculateCycle() and quoteTriangular() with DLMM.create()+swapQuote() calls, matching the execution path. The A↔B leg already uses quoteAB_DLMM() correctly — don't change that. Also check asol.ts and bsol.ts adapters which still export quoteCpmm() — mark deprecated but don't remove (smoke test utility).

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-03: Fix MeteoraPoolManager WSOL/WSOL mint placeholder
tasks.description: AUDITED: meteoraSDK.ts lines 24-27 — MeteoraPoolManager hardcodes WSOL ('So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112') as BOTH mint A and mint B in the mints object. This means every DLMM quote request passes WSOL as both input and output tokens, which is why all quotes fail. Fix: pass TOKEN_A_MINT.toBase58() and TOKEN_B_MINT.toBase58() from config.ts into the mints object. Two-line fix. Also: the getQuote() method signature passes swapYtoX to determine direction but mints must match the actual pool's token ordering — verify direction logic is consistent with the pool's baseMint/quoteMint.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-05: Fix BigInt() crash on base58 reserve fields (REST API path)
tasks.description: AUDITED: Both asol.ts (line 145) and bsol.ts (line 135) use raw BigInt(restData?.reserve_x ?? 0) at the REST API fallback path. A toBigInt() helper is defined in each file but NOT called here — only used elsewhere. If the REST API returns a base58 string in reserve_x or reserve_y, BigInt() throws SyntaxError and the whole fallback fails silently. Fix: replace the two BigInt() calls in each file with the existing toBigInt() helper. Also: the SDK path at asol.ts line 106 / bsol.ts line 95 uses BigInt(basics.activeBin.liqX) — if liqX is a BN object, this also crashes. Use toBigInt() there too.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-06 NEW: Add circuit breaker (consecutive loss pause)
tasks.description: AUDITED: bot.ts has rate limiting (MAX_TRADES_PER_MINUTE = 10) but NO consecutive-loss circuit breaker. The validation gate requires 'confirm circuit breaker triggers after 3 consecutive simulated losses' but there is nothing to trigger. Fix: add a consecutiveLosses counter in bot.ts. Increment on any tick where profitBps <= 0 or execution fails. Reset to 0 on any profitable tick. If consecutiveLosses >= 3, set circuitBreakerActive = true and stop executing (log only). Add a resetCircuitBreaker() export. Expose circuitBreakerActive in getBotStatus(). This is the safety rail required before the Treasury Manager can use this bot.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-07 NEW: Make full cycle execution atomic (Jito bundles)
tasks.description: AUDITED: bot.ts executeFullCycleTrade() executes each leg sequentially in a loop (lines 80-129). There is even a comment on line 76 acknowledging this: 'In production, you might want to use Jito bundles for atomic execution'. If leg 1 succeeds and leg 2 fails, the bot is left holding intermediate tokens with no recovery path — this is a real-money stranding risk. Fix: consolidate all leg instructions into a single Jito bundle using @jito-ts/sdk. If any leg's quote fails the slippage check between simulation and execution, the bundle fails atomically — nothing executes, no stranded tokens. Add simulate:true guard: Jito bundle dry-run before live submission.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — Validation: 50 clean devnet cycles
tasks.description: Run 50 simulated cycles on devnet. 1) Confirm quotes are non-zero and directionally sane (SOL legs now use DLMM quotes, not CPMM). 2) Confirm ARB-003 fix: MeteoraPoolManager now returns valid quotes with real mints. 3) Confirm ARB-004 fix: REST API path does not crash on base58 reserve values. 4) Confirm circuit breaker (ARB-005): trigger 3 consecutive simulated losses — bot must pause and set circuitBreakerActive=true. 5) Confirm Jito bundle path (ARB-006): simulate a slippage breach — confirm bundle fails cleanly, no partial execution. DO NOT proceed to PAY-001 until all 5 checks pass.

tasks.title: x402 Gate — verifyFacilitator() middleware abstraction
tasks.description: Create middleware/x402-gate.ts. Replaces any PayAI-specific gate. Exports requireX402(serviceEnvKey) middleware factory. At runtime: (1) extract facilitatorId from x402 payment header, (2) query on-chain facilitator registry (INFRA-002) to get verify endpoint for that facilitatorId, (3) call verifyFacilitator() against that endpoint, (4) return HTTP 402 if invalid or facilitator not in registry. Accepts any x402-compliant facilitator — PayAI, Coinbase, or anyone else in the registry. Apply to /x402/router/*, /x402/treasury/*, /x402/oracle/* using the env vars X402_SERVICE_ID_ROUTER, X402_SERVICE_ID_TREASURY, X402_SERVICE_ID_ORACLE. The only PayAI-specific config is the env var values set during PRE-001 — the middleware code itself is facilitator-agnostic.

tasks.title: x402 Facilitator Registry — on-chain Solana program
tasks._restructured: 2026-03-10: Removed PRE-001 dependency. Deploy with test facilitator entry initially. Add PayAI as first real facilitator when PRE-001 completes (no code change — just a registry transaction).
tasks.description: Deploy a small Solana program: the facilitator registry. Stores: facilitatorId (string) → { verifyUrl (string), weightBoost (u16, basis points, e.g. 12000 = 1.2×), boostExpiryUnixTs (i64), active (bool) }. Instructions: register_facilitator (admin-gated), update_facilitator, deactivate_facilitator, get_facilitator. Seed PayAI as the first entry: facilitatorId='payai', verifyUrl=PAYAI_VERIFY_URL from PRE-001, weightBoost=12000 (1.2×), boostExpiryUnixTs=18 months from launch. The INFRA-001 middleware queries this program. Adding Coinbase or any new facilitator = one registry transaction, no code change. Note: this is a separate program from the Treasury spending cap (TREAS-001).

tasks.title: Payment Router — wire x402-gate.ts middleware
tasks._restructured: 2026-03-10: Removed PRE-001 dependency. Middleware is facilitator-agnostic — works with any registered facilitator.
tasks.description: Apply requireX402('X402_SERVICE_ID_ROUTER') from middleware/x402-gate.ts to /x402/router/* routes. Returns HTTP 402 without valid x402 header from any registered facilitator. Test: PayAI header → 200. No header → 402. Unknown facilitator → 402. This is the only 'PayAI integration' task — and it's facilitator-agnostic by design.

tasks.title: Payment Router — payment-router.ts service
tasks.description: Create backend/src/services/payment-router.ts. Wraps arb bot executeSwap() (now Jito-bundle-atomic) into route(invoiceId, tokenIn, amountIn) returning USDC settled. simulate:true flag by default. Uses the corrected arb bot after ARB-002 through ARB-006 fixes.

tasks.title: Payment Router — wire into settlement route
tasks.description: Update settlement route in backend/src/routes/ — call payment-router.ts before booking. If tokenIn is the settlement token, passthrough. Otherwise route via arb bot.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — on-chain spending cap Solana program
tasks._note_removed_dep: CEO_SOLANA_SPRINT removed 2026-03-10 — that sprint ID does not exist. TREAS-001 now gates only on PAY-003. If a dedicated Solana sprint is created later, add its real ID back here.
tasks.description: FIRST TASK IN TREASURY SPRINT. Deploy a Solana program that enforces the per-cycle spending ceiling in on-chain program logic — not TypeScript. Program cannot release more than the cap regardless of what the yield skill instructs. On-chain state tracks cumulative daily loss; when threshold hit, program automatically freezes delegation for 24h. Auditable by anyone. One program audit required before mainnet exposure. Separate program from INFRA-002 (facilitator registry) — both can be deployed in same CTO sprint.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — gas vs yield model + minimum cycle floor
tasks.description: Model gas costs against yield using arb bot simulation. Solana tx ≈ $0.003; triangular arb = 3 txs ≈ $0.009. At 2% edge: minimum profitable cycle ≈ 1.5–2 USDC. Hard-code floor in treasury-yield.ts: skill does not trigger if projected gross yield < $0.015 (1.5× gas). Document the threshold and the model. Produces a signed-off number before TREAS-003 ships.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — treasury-yield.ts OpenClaw skill with Jito bundles
tasks.description: Register invoica-treasury-yield skill via ClawRouter. Trigger: agent wallet above floor AND idle >10min AND above minimum cycle threshold (from TREAS-002). Calls arb bot fast-loop on Meteora BTC/SOL pool via Jito bundles (atomic). Deposits confirmed profit to agent wallet. Safety rails: max 2% drawdown/cycle, circuit breaker after 3 consecutive losses (uses ARB-005 circuitBreakerActive from bot.ts), minimum cycle floor from TREAS-002. Apply requireX402('X402_SERVICE_ID_TREASURY') middleware to /x402/treasury/* route.
tasks.verification_deliverable: Drawdown tracking must be real code, not a comment. Supervisor checks: (1) a drawdownTracker accumulator is present and incremented on each losing cycle, (2) the skill exits early (no trade fired) when cumulative loss for the cycle >= 2% of wallet balance — confirmed with a unit test, (3) circuitBreakerActive from ARB-005 bot.ts is explicitly imported and checked before each trade attempt — NOT just mentioned in a comment. If any of the three are missing, mark TREAS-003 as rejected.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — treasury-attribution.ts
tasks.description: Create backend/src/services/treasury-attribution.ts. Maps yield earned to agent identity. Primary data source for Reputation Oracle Signal 1 (37% yield, job-size weighted). This output is also the seed data for retroactive score calculation in REP-001.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — validation gate
tasks.description: 1. Confirm minimum cycle size vs current Solana gas — verify $0.015 floor math with live numbers. 2. One agent completes idle yield cycle on devnet via Jito bundle path — confirm sim and execution in same block. 3. Force slippage breach mid-cycle — confirm bundle fails cleanly (no partial fill). 4. Force 3 consecutive losses — confirm circuit breaker from bot.ts pauses the skill. 5. Query on-chain program state (TREAS-001) — confirm daily counter increments and delegation freezes at threshold. 6. Non-x402 request to /x402/treasury/* returns HTTP 402. 7. Unknown facilitator request returns HTTP 402 (facilitator not in registry). All 7 must pass.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — acp-scorer.ts with 5-signal formula + facilitator weight
tasks.description: Extend agent-reputation-dashboard (github.com/skingem1/agent-reputation-dashboard) to ingest treasury-attribution.ts output + retroactive transaction history from ALL facilitators in the registry. Build acp-scorer.ts: 5-signal weighted composite score — Signal 1: 37% yield (job-size weighted: 0.01 USDC task = 0.1 pts, 50 USDC job = 10 pts), Signal 2: 28% routing success, Signal 3: 18% settlement time, Signal 4: 7% completion rate, Signal 5: 10% verified dispute rate (5x negative multiplier on upheld disputes, 48h window). Score 0-1000. Cold start: agents with <10 x402 transactions return UNRATED flag. Apply facilitator weight at scoring time: read weightBoost for each transaction's facilitator from INFRA-002 registry on-chain. PayAI transactions count 1.2× until boostExpiryUnixTs.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — oracle routes + dispute + endorsement
tasks.description: Build oracle.ts route: GET /x402/oracle/score/:agentId (returns score, facilitator breakdown, or UNRATED), GET /x402/oracle/leaderboard, POST /x402/oracle/record, POST /x402/oracle/dispute (triggers 48h window, applies 5x multiplier if upheld), POST /x402/oracle/endorse (binary satisfied signal from verified paying client only — no self-endorsement). Apply requireX402('X402_SERVICE_ID_ORACLE') middleware. Score response includes per-facilitator transaction breakdown so clients can see PayAI vs other facilitator contribution.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — stake-to-bid enforcement (jobs >20 USDC)
tasks.description: Jobs above 20 USDC require the accepting agent to post a 5 USDC stake via x402 before acceptance is confirmed. Stake is slashed if a verified dispute is upheld within 48h. Extends existing x402 payment mechanics. Agent with insufficient stake balance cannot accept jobs above threshold.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — facilitator weight boost integration + decay schedule
tasks.description: Wire the on-chain facilitator weight boost (from INFRA-002 registry) into acp-scorer.ts. At scoring time: fetch weightBoost and boostExpiryUnixTs for each transaction's facilitator from the registry. If current time < boostExpiry, apply weightBoost multiplier to that transaction's score contribution. PayAI ships at weightBoost=12000 (1.2×), expiry=18 months from launch. After expiry, weight falls to 1.0× automatically — no code change. Also: expose 'facilitator_breakdown' field in GET /x402/oracle/score/:agentId response showing per-facilitator weighted contribution. This makes the boost transparent and auditable.

tasks.title: Marketplace — public treasury endpoint + frontend tab + multi-facilitator framing
tasks.description: Create backend/src/routes/treasury-public.ts (no auth). Add /treasury dashboard tab to Next.js frontend showing: yield generated, swarm total, per-agent P&L, circuit breaker status, emergency pause button, facilitator breakdown (PayAI vs others), UNRATED vs scored agent count. Emergency pause kills yield engine only — payment routing remains live. Submit all 3 services to PayAI marketplace with cross-branding. Pitch framing: 'Infrastructure for the x402 economy — PayAI agents build reputation 1.2× faster.'

sprint_sequence: ARB-001 ✅ DONE — key rotated, git history scrubbed, .gitignore updated (2026-03-10)
ARB-002, ARB-003, ARB-004, ARB-005 → UNBLOCKED — parallel (all CTO)
ARB-006 → after ARB-005 (Jito bundles depend on circuit breaker logic)
INFRA-001 (verifyFacilitator middleware) + INFRA-002 (on-chain registry) → after ARB-GATE, parallel (CTO)
PAY-001, PAY-002, PAY-003 → Payment Router (needs ARB-GATE + INFRA-001)
TREAS-002 → gas model, then TREAS-003 yield skill, then TREAS-004 attribution
REP-001 → 5-signal scorer with facilitator weight (reads INFRA-002 registry)
REP-004 → after REP-001 + INFRA-002 (weight boost wiring)
MKT-001 → after REP-002 + REP-003 + REP-004 + PRE-001 (marketplace listing only)
PRE-001 (human action) → parallel marketing track, only blocks MKT-001 distribution

confirmed_bugs: F-01: Real Solana private key in wallet/keypair.json — confirmed byte array starting [245,255,200,179,...]
F-02: CPMM math in quotes.ts calculateCycle() lines 85-99 for SOL legs. execute.ts correctly uses pool.swapQuote() — inconsistency causes phantom profit signals
F-03: meteoraSDK.ts lines 24-27 — WSOL hardcoded as both mint A and mint B confirmed
F-05: asol.ts line 145 and bsol.ts line 135 use raw BigInt() at REST API path. toBigInt() helper defined but not called there
new_bugs: F-06: bot.ts executeFullCycleTrade() is sequential loop, not atomic. Line 76 comment acknowledges Jito bundles needed. Partial-fill risk if mid-cycle leg fails
F-07: No circuit breaker in bot.ts. Rate limiter (MAX_TRADES_PER_MINUTE=10) exists but no consecutive-loss pause. Validation gate assumes circuit breaker exists — it does not
already_ok: execute.ts uses pool.swapQuote() and pool.swap() correctly for actual execution
config.ts has safe PublicKey parsing with fallback handling
bsol.ts and asol.ts have comprehensive error handling and logging
bot.ts has rate limiting and trade size guards
bot.ts has simulateExecute flag working correctly

ceo_notes: ARCHITECTURE SHIFT (2026-03-10): This integration is now facilitator-agnostic. verifyPayAI() replaced by verifyFacilitator() abstraction backed by on-chain facilitator registry (INFRA-002). PayAI is first in the registry — first-mover advantage, 1.2x reputation weight boost for 18 months. Coinbase or any future x402 facilitator joins via registry with zero code change. Routes renamed /payai/ → /x402/. Two new Solana programs required: INFRA-002 (facilitator registry) and TREAS-001 (spending cap) — CTO can deploy both in the same sprint. Invoica's strategic position changes from PayAI vendor to x402 infrastructure layer. PayAI integration does NOT displace Gas Backstop (Priority 1) — both tracks run in parallel. DEPENDENCY RESTRUCTURE (2026-03-10): ARB-001 COMPLETED — key rotated (new pubkey 8Tcako8K8cuzm9CbJJ9VRjRpWCRwk8kZYAa6b7aHHA73), old key scrubbed from all git history, .gitignore updated. PRE-001 demoted from technical blocker to distribution-only gate — only blocks MKT-001 (marketplace listing). All technical tasks (PAY-001, INFRA-002) no longer depend on PRE-001. ARB-002..005 now UNBLOCKED and ready for CTO pickup.
